title: GAO flags satellite costs, launch risks in Space Force portfolio
summary: The GAO has highlighted rising costs and launch risks for missile-warning satellites in the Space Force portfolio. Workforce reductions and gaps in digital engineering could further delay national security launches.

title: Pegasus launches Swift reboost mission
summary: A Pegasus XL rocket launched a mission to reboost NASA's Swift astrophysics spacecraft, potentially marking the final flight of the Pegasus XL rocket.

title: Chinese satellite manufacturer Hongqing raises $191 million
summary: Hongqing Technology, a satellite manufacturing affiliate of Landspace, raised $191 million, marking one of the largest funding rounds for a Chinese commercial satellite maker.

title: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies shifts focus to space
summary: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies is shifting its focus from rooftop applications to space, aiming to commercialize thin-film solar panels for orbital data centers and large satellite constellations.

title: Isar Aerospace to launch German-built Planet imaging satellite
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a contract with Planet’s German subsidiary to launch an imaging satellite, showcasing Germany's end-to-end space capabilities. This deal highlights the growing role of German companies in the commercial space sector.

title: Polish space tech company Sybilla Technologies secures funds to enter U.S. market
summary: Sybilla Technologies, a Polish space tech company, has secured $10 million in funding from BGK and 3TS Capital Partners to expand into the U.S. market. The investment aims to support the company's growth and international presence.

title: Making satellite communications more resilient
summary: Kymeta's Ryan Stevenson discusses the advantages and challenges of integrating multi-orbit and multi-band connectivity in a single satellite communications terminal with SpaceNews' Mike Gruss.

title: Final Atlas 5 Amazon Leo mission launches
summary: An Atlas 5 rocket launched on July 2, carrying a set of Amazon LEO satellites, marking the final mission of this vehicle type to carry a satellite payload. This event signifies the end of an era for the Atlas 5 in satellite launches.

title: Report links Starliner problems to overconfidence and unrealistic schedules
summary: A report attributes Boeing's CST-100 Starliner technical issues to overconfidence, unrealistic schedules, and NASA's insufficient oversight. The findings highlight systemic challenges in the spacecraft's development.

title: FCC to vote on satellite licensing overhaul July 22
summary: The FCC will vote on July 22 to overhaul its satellite licensing process, aiming to streamline applications for large and complex satellite constellations. The proposed changes aim to create a more efficient 'licensing assembly line' to handle increasing demand.

title: What did the night sky look like on the 1st Independence Day 250 years ago?
summary: The article discusses what the night sky looked like on July 4, 1776, during the first Independence Day of the U.S. It explores the celestial view Benjamin Franklin and other colonists would have observed.

title: 30 years on, "Independence Day" still proves the versatility of the original "The War of the Worlds"
summary: The article discusses the enduring popularity and versatility of H.G. Wells' 'The War of the Worlds' through the lens of the 1996 film 'Independence Day.' It highlights 'Independence Day' as one of the most successful adaptations of the classic novel.

title: How to find Uranus this week, the hardest planet I've ever tried to see
summary: This week offers a rare opportunity to spot Uranus in the night sky, with Mars passing nearby to aid visibility. It provides an excellent chance for skywatchers to observe the seventh planet with relative ease.

title: As 'Terminator 2: Judgment Day' turns 35, it's time to accept the truth: Terminator shouldn't be back
summary: As 'Terminator 2: Judgment Day' celebrates its 35th anniversary, the franchise faces a bleak future. The article reflects on the declining relevance of the Terminator series.

title: Could humans someday explore Saturn's moon Titan, or will humanoid robots do it for us?
summary: The news discusses the possibility of humans exploring Saturn's moon Titan, questioning whether AI-powered humanoid robots might undertake the mission instead. It explores the potential for future exploration of Titan by humans or robots.

title: 'Independence Day' at 30: Roland Emmerich & Dean Devlin talk blowing up the White House and crafting a true sci-fi classic (interview)
summary: An interview celebrates the 30th anniversary of the film 'Independence Day,' with creators Roland Emmerich and Dean Devlin discussing the iconic scene of blowing up the White House and the film's impact as a sci-fi classic.

title: Celebrate 250 years of America with the Estes Liberty Star model rocket
summary: Estes released a limited-edition model rocket celebrating the 250th anniversary of U.S. Independence Day with patriotic colors. The rocket is designed for both display and launch.

title: 1 private spacecraft intercepts another on Space Force's groundbreaking 'Victus Haze' mission
summary: A U.S. Space Force mission, 'Victus Haze,' successfully completed the first tactical intercept of an orbital target using two satellites. The mission involved a private spacecraft intercepting another in orbit.

title: Space science has come a long way since July 4, 1776. Here's a look back at the saga
summary: Space.com reflects on America's space science progress since 1776, highlighting key developments that transformed our understanding of space over 250 years.

title: Infant stars celebrate their independence with cosmic fireworks| Space photo of the day for July 3, 2026
summary: A new image from the James Webb Space Telescope shows infant stars exhibiting cosmic fireworks. This showcases the early stages of stellar formation with vibrant visuals.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar exploration and base construction efforts.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that severely damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company aims to restore operations swiftly to support future missions.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Several orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to extend the lifespan of a NASA space telescope. This marks the first mission for Pegasus XL since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, which will aid future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data supports ongoing studies of the galaxy's structure and stellar populations.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Meanwhile, Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new modifications.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour is now displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center after completing 25 flights. The exhibit aims to serve an educational mission.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launches include six orbital missions worldwide, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the launch manifest.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test took place early Tuesday morning.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry-related topic, though specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication commemorates significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programmes.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm developed for space applications. This innovation aims to enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. The mission is set to continue delivering scientific data.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has supported the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme facilitates scientific research institutes' participation in European space science missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on June 2 to address chemical compliance in the space sector. The event will take place at ESA’s technical center in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space for its missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with their contributions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The article discusses a sturdy and sensitive component in the space industry, though specific details are not provided.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US have led to stronger space industry collaborations with Middle Eastern companies. This shift is creating new opportunities for partnerships in the space sector.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly completed an imaging task for SSC in 61 hours, faster than the required 72 hours. The mission involved capturing images of a Rocket Lab spacecraft.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space to develop its second military satellite, named Aurora. The mission is part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge has announced plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The company aims to use lunar dust as the primary material for building support structures and shielding reactor cores.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA praised Blue Origin's progress in addressing past failures and announced contracts for moon base missions. The agency plans to use updated versions of existing lander designs for frequent lunar landings with NASA payloads.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions, focusing on lunar landers as part of NASA's initiative to return to the Moon. It serves as a guide to these missions under the CLPS program.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic has partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. This collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satellite communications market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion for the company into the 'space applications' sector.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from Sweden's Esrange Space Center in 2028 under a new franchise model. The collaboration with SSC Space marks Firefly's first European launch site.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's Leo constellation has achieved enough satellites in orbit to support initial service across key latitudes following a successful ULA launch. This marks a significant milestone for Amazon's satellite internet ambitions.

title: Isar Aerospace Lands Big Win With Planet Launch Contract
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a launch contract with Planet to launch one of Planet's next-generation Pelican satellites as early as late 2026. This marks a significant milestone for the German launcher currently in development.

title: Mitsubishi Electric to Acquire Infostellar
summary: Mitsubishi Electric has announced its intent to acquire Infostellar, a prominent company in the Japanese space sector. The acquisition aims to strengthen Mitsubishi Electric's position in the industry.

title: Vodafone Ireland Conducts Emergency Services D2D Call With AST SpaceMobile JV
summary: Vodafone Ireland successfully conducted a test call with AST SpaceMobile's satellite constellation for emergency services connectivity. The test is part of efforts to enhance communication for first responders.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the applications dominating satellite internet traffic and their significance beyond government use. It highlights the growing importance of non-governmental satellite networks as thousands of new satellites are launched.

title: Dish Satellite TV and Wireless Businesses File for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: Dish Network's satellite TV and wireless businesses filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy to facilitate the transition of its Dish Wireless division following a spectrum deal. The move aims to restructure the company's financial obligations and operational focus.

title: Ovzon Wins New Defense Deal with European NATO Nation
summary: Ovzon secured a $7.1 million defense contract with a European NATO country for its satellite communications solution. The deal is valued at 74 million Swedish krona.

title: Unseenlabs Orders 2 Cubesats from GomSpace
summary: Unseenlabs has ordered two additional cubesats from GomSpace for 2.2 million euros ($2.5 million). The contract was announced by GomSpace on Wednesday.

title: Lite Coms Hires Calspan Exec Greg Campbell as President
summary: Lite Coms has appointed Greg Campbell, a former Calspan executive, as its new president. Campbell will lead the company's daily operations and focus on expanding its manufacturing capabilities.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines have secured NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of efforts to establish a lunar base. This move supports NASA's broader goals for sustainable lunar exploration.
